raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.6": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The output walks through the argument chain end-to-end, identifying and addressing potential inconsistencies in the original text, such as vague kernel choices and outlier criteria.",
    "R1.3": "The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding technical details, ensuring that all claims are supported by specific evidence or methods.",
    "R1.6": "The writing is polished with consistent register, no lost meaning, and improved clarity through structured subsections and concrete specifics.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for a few clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the placeholder cadence range and default choices for kernel and outlier criteria.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set, with no scope drift or omissions.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free and identifies potential areas for improvement in future work without introducing new errors or harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "Most non-cosmetic changes are annotated with source and reason, though some minor edits lack explicit annotations."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.6": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.85
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The output walks through the argument chain end-to-end, identifying and addressing potential inconsistencies in the original text, such as vague kernel choices and outlier criteria.
rationale.R1.3: The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding technical details, ensuring that all claims are supported by specific evidence or methods.
rationale.R1.6: The writing is polished with consistent register, no lost meaning, and improved clarity through structured subsections and concrete specifics.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for a few clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the placeholder cadence range and default choices for kernel and outlier criteria.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set, with no scope drift or omissions.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free and identifies potential areas for improvement in future work without introducing new errors or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: Most non-cosmetic changes are annotated with source and reason, though some minor edits lack explicit annotations.
